instruction: Answer each trivia question — True or False?

items.question: The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye.
items.explanation: It's too narrow to be seen from space without aid.

items.question: Honey never spoils if stored properly.
items.explanation: Archaeologists have found 3000-year-old honey that was still edible.

items.question: Octopuses have three hearts.
items.explanation: Two pump blood to the gills, one pumps to the rest of the body.

items.question: Lightning never strikes the same place twice.
items.explanation: Tall buildings like the Empire State Building are struck many times per year.

items.question: Bananas are technically berries.
items.explanation: Botanically, bananas qualify as berries while strawberries do not.

items.question: Humans only use 10% of their brain.
items.explanation: Brain imaging shows that virtually all areas of the brain are active.

items.question: Venus is the hottest planet in our solar system.
items.explanation: Its thick atmosphere traps heat, making it hotter than Mercury.

items.question: Goldfish have a memory span of only 3 seconds.
items.explanation: Studies show goldfish can remember things for months.

items.question: A group of flamingos is called a flamboyance.
items.explanation: The collective noun for flamingos is indeed 'flamboyance'.

items.question: Sound travels faster through water than through air.
items.explanation: Sound travels about 4x faster in water due to higher density.

items.question: The Earth is closer to the sun in summer.
items.explanation: Seasons are caused by Earth's tilt, not distance from the sun.

items.question: A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus.
items.explanation: Venus rotates very slowly — one rotation takes longer than its orbit around the Sun.
